mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher financing costs, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when the federal funds rate is rising, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5 percent signals severe demand contraction that immediately triggers equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces delayed policy tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation low-rate regime produces positive 60-day forward returns in over 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth exceeding 200,000 jobs during a post-shock recovery phase confirms economic stabilization, driving risk-on flows and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 200,000 fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with a negative Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect expanding manufacturing output that directly supports corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with industrial production growth over a 3-year out-of-sample period.
